fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to ohio, respiratory therapist compact ohio, ohio respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Ohio Respiratory Care Board
board.mailing_address: 30 East Broad Street, 3rd Floor, Columbus, OH 43215-6120

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Ohio.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Ohio Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Navigate to elicense.ohio.gov, register a new account, and complete the online application for a Respiratory Care Professional license. This includes providing demographic information, educational history, and answering eligibility questions.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited respiratory care program to be sent directly to the State Medical Board of Ohio.

steps.title: Provide NBRC Scores
steps.description: Ensure your National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) scores, demonstrating passage of the RRT credentialing requirements (Therapist Multiple-Choice (TMC) Examination at the RRT cut score and the Clinical Simulation Examination (CSE), or the RRT examination), are sent directly to the Board by authorizing NBRC to release them.

steps.title: Complete a Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Arrange for a fingerprint-based criminal background check. Ohio residents typically use WebCheck, while out-of-state applicants use physical fingerprint cards processed through the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation and Identification (BCI&I).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable $125 application fee through the eLicense Ohio portal as part of your online application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Ohio respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Ohio. To work in Ohio, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Ohio
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Professional license in Ohio, you must complete an online application through the State Medical Board of Ohio's **eLicense Ohio** portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the eLicense Ohio portal (elicense.ohio.gov). 2. Click 'Register a New Account' and follow the prompts to create your user ID and password. 3. Log in to your new account and select 'Apply for a New License' to begin the Respiratory Care Professional application.
